untranslatable_reason: Diamond pattern detection requires identifying higher highs/lower lows followed by lower highs/higher lows with trend line analysis. This requires multi-bar pattern recognition (comparing sequences of highs and lows across multiple bars and fitting trend lines) which cannot be expressed using only point-in-time indicator comparisons and the allowed operators (>, <, >=, <=, ==, crosses_above, crosses_below, is). The pattern is inherently a visual/structural recognition task requiring custom pattern detection logic.
translation_notes: Attempted to capture volume component of the signal (diminishing volume during formation, high volume on breakout) as a proxy, but this does not adequately represent the core diamond pattern recognition requirement. The signal requires: (1) identification of higher highs and lower lows in sequence, (2) subsequent identification of lower highs and higher lows in sequence, (3) visual/geometric confirmation that trend lines form a diamond shape. None of these can be implemented with the allowed indicator set and operators.
